question: In Jesus’ trial scene, why does the ‘Son of Man seated at the right hand’ + ‘coming with the clouds’ combo trigger the charge-worthy reaction—and what OT texts are fused there?

choices.A: It fuses Daniel’s cloud-coming Son of Man with Psalm 110’s enthroned figure, implying exalted authority that provokes a blasphemy verdict.
choices.B: It is a harmless metaphor about weather.
choices.C: It means Jesus denies any future judgment.
choices.D: It proves Jesus is uncertain and changing his story.

teaching.title: A deliberate OT fusion at the trial
teaching.body: The cloud-coming imagery in Daniel 7 signals a figure receiving divine-level authority, while Psalm 110 speaks of enthronement at God’s right hand. When Jesus combines them, the leaders hear a claim that goes beyond messiah-as-political-king. The reaction in the narrative is part of the argument: they understand the implication.
